title: changing from night sleep to wake up doesn’t work
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

changing from night sleep to wake up doesn’t work

---

**Category:** `bug` `sleep`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
implementation_notes: Fixed in src/tabs/LogTab.tsx: the sleep entry submission logic was incorrectly overriding 'Wake Up' as the type, treating it the same as a missing type. Changed the condition from `!entry.type || entry.type === 'Wake Up'` to just `!entry.type`, so Wake Up is preserved as a valid explicit type. Added unit test in tests/unit/sleepWakeUpTransition.test.ts covering night sleep → wake up and day sleep → wake up transitions. Also added vitest as a dev dependency (package.json / package-lock.json).
enhanced_description:
The user reported that transitioning a sleep entry from 'night sleep' to 'wake up' does not work.

What was reported:
When the baby is currently in a 'night sleep' session and the user tries to mark the baby as awake (i.e., end the night sleep session / change state to 'wake up'), the action has no effect — the sleep state remains stuck as 'night sleep'.

Component/area affected:
Sleep tracking feature — likely the sleep state machine, the SleepTracker or SleepLog component, and/or the associated hook/store that manages the active sleep session type.

Expected behaviour:
Tapping or triggering 'wake up' while in a night sleep session should end the night sleep session, record the end time and total duration, and update the UI to reflect the awake state.

Current behaviour:
The transition from night sleep → awake does not occur. The UI either shows no change or the button has no effect.

Edge cases to handle:
- Verify whether the same bug affects day sleep → wake up transitions (may share the same code path)
- Check if the bug is in the event handler (button click), the state update logic, or the persistence layer (IndexedDB write)
- Confirm the night sleep entry is still openable/editable after the failed transition
- Check for any conditional logic that gates the wake-up action (e.g., minimum duration guard, wrong sleep type check)

Acceptance criteria:
- User can successfully end a night sleep session by tapping 'wake up'
- The night sleep end time is recorded and duration is calculated correctly
- The UI updates immediately to reflect the awake state
- No regression for day sleep → wake up transitions

Testing:
- Add a unit test reproducing the bug: start a night sleep session, trigger wake-up, assert the session ends and state transitions to awake
- Add a regression test covering the day sleep → wake up path to confirm no regression
- Test persistence: after wake-up, verify the sleep entry in IndexedDB has a valid end time and duration
